identity.canonical_title: Practical Next Steps I Can Execute Now (Pick Any or Several): R=O(r/R)

theory.canonical_statement: **(a) Exact Laplace–Beltrami on torus & eigenmode residuals** - Derive \(\Delta_s\) in (φ,θ) coordinates and compute \(R(\phi,\theta)=\Delta_s\Psi_{2,1} + \lambda\Psi_{2,1}\). Show asymptotic bound \(R=O(r/R)\). → This verifies Step 2 as *approximate eigenmode*.
theory.technical_interpretation: Practical Next Steps I Can Execute Now (Pick Any or Several): R=O(r/R) is an algebraic definition, written R=O(r/R). It is an algebraic definition expressing the quantity directly in terms of the variables on its right-hand side. It is built from O, r, defining R. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.
